growth_records.strain: S. elongatus PCC7942 cscB+; E. coli K-12 MG1655; E. coli W; B. subtilis str. 168
growth_records.medium: Coculture minimal media (CoBBG-11 / CoYBG-11, BG-11 based) with 2% sucrose

up.method: heterotrophs consume phototroph-secreted sucrose (except E. coli K-12 which lacks a sucrose transporter and lives on other exchanged metabolites); rate not numerically resolved

growth_records.cite: Zuñiga et al. Synthetic microbial communities of heterotrophs and phototrophs facilitate sustainable growth. Nature Communications (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41467-020-17612-8. [PMC7393147]
growth_records.snippet: Experimental growth rates of phototrophs and heterotrophs ranged between 0.04 ± 0.01 h−1 and 0.06 ± 0.015 h−1, similar to predicted growth rates of 0.05 h−1 and 0.07 h−1
growth_records.notes: Aggregate experimental growth-rate range across SPC prokaryotic members (phototroph + heterotroph): 0.04 +/- 0.01 to 0.06 +/- 0.015 h-1; per-organism values not individually tabulated in text (Fig 1b source data external). Recorded mu=0.05 h-1 as the midpoint representative; doubling_time = ln2/0.05 = 13.9 h (calculated). Predicted CM-model growth rates 0.05 and 0.07 h-1. Rich-medium (80% LB + 20% BG-11 + 0.4% glucose) raised community/monoculture growth up to 41% (no absolute numbers given). This record aggregates multiple prokaryotic members because the paper does not resolve them numerically; retained at low confidence for the growth-rate range only. [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature, pH]
